title: pellaea hastata
intro: Pellaea hastata, also known as the mountain bracken, is a type of fern native to the mountainous regions of southern Africa. The medicinal properties of Pellaea hastata include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Pellaea hastata include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Pellaea hastata can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
